items.headline: US Stocks Fall on Weak Jobs Data and Surging Oil Prices
items.summary: Major U.S. stock indices experienced declines today, driven by a disappointing jobs report indicating a loss of 92,000 nonfarm payrolls in February, and a significant surge in oil prices amidst escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Investor sentiment is broadly risk-averse, with concerns mounting over a potential mix of weak economic growth and rising inflation.

items.headline: Circle Internet Group Rises Amid Crypto Bill Discussions
items.summary: Circle Internet Group saw a significant gain as crypto stocks experienced a volatile week, influenced by headlines surrounding a stalled crypto bill.

items.headline: Coinbase Global Gains on Crypto Market Volatility
items.summary: Coinbase Global also posted a notable increase, with crypto markets reacting to developments around a potential crypto bill.

items.headline: Day One Biopharmaceuticals Surges
items.summary: Day One Biopharmaceuticals experienced a sharp increase in its stock price, with investors continuing to seek growth opportunities.

items.headline: Rocket Companies Slumps on AI Partnership News
items.summary: Rocket Companies saw a significant drop after Better.com announced a partnership with OpenAI to launch a conversational credit decision engine in ChatGPT.

items.headline: Nomura Holdings Retreats Amid Middle East Conflict
items.summary: Japanese banking giant Nomura Holdings was among the week's biggest losers, with Japan's market particularly affected by volatility stemming from the Middle East conflict.

items.headline: Calidi Biotherapeutics Experiences Steep Decline
items.summary: Calidi Biotherapeutics, Inc. registered a substantial decrease in its share value.

items.headline: Weak Jobs Report Fuels Rate Cut Speculation
items.summary: A monthly jobs report revealed that U.S. nonfarm payrolls fell by 92,000 in February, contrary to expectations, and the unemployment rate also rose. This data is intensifying discussions around a potential softening of the Federal Reserve's policy and the timing of future rate cuts. The U.S. is currently in a 'quiet period' for Fed representatives ahead of upcoming meetings.

items.headline: Oil Prices Surge Amid Escalating Middle East Tensions
items.summary: Oil prices have surged significantly, with Brent crude rocketing to $93 per barrel and WTI crude jumping above $89 per barrel, primarily due to escalating conflict in the Middle East. The killing of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has heightened war escalations and raised concerns about global oil supply chains and energy security. This surge is contributing to fears of a stagflationary environment.

items.headline: Energy Sector Leads While Basic Materials and Financials Lag
items.summary: The energy sector emerged as a relative leader this week, up 1.32%. Conversely, the basic materials sector performed worst, declining by 7.37% for the week. Financials also continued to extend losses for the second consecutive week. Airline stocks notably fell sharply as jet fuel prices doubled.

items.headline: Target Stock Rallies Despite Sales Decline, Forecasts 2026 Growth
items.summary: Target ($TGT) stock surged after releasing its latest earnings report. Despite a 2% net sales decline in fiscal 2025 and a 9.7% drop in net income, management forecasts a 2% net sales growth for fiscal 2026 and plans to invest $1 billion in store updates and AI.

items.headline: Micron, Adobe, Oracle Poised for Potential Earnings Beats
items.summary: Prediction markets are strongly favoring earnings beats for several major tech and software companies, including Micron ($MU), Adobe ($ADBE), and Oracle ($ORCL). Oracle is scheduled to report earnings on March 10, 2026, followed by Adobe on March 12, 2026.

items.headline: Genesco Inc. Reports Strong Q4 and Full-Year Results
items.summary: Genesco Inc. ($GCO) reported robust fourth-quarter and full fiscal year results for the period ended January 31, 2026. The company saw a 7% increase in net sales for Q4 and 9% comparable sales growth, marking its sixth consecutive quarter of positive comparable sales growth.
